new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a grape must that has been concentrated (e.g. by evaporation or other physical means), as distinguished from unfermented or partially fermented grape must that has not been concentrated.

Other grape must (CN 2204 30), other than grape must in fermentation or with fermentation arrested otherwise than by addition of alcohol, that has been concentrated (see additional note 7 to Chapter 22). Corresponds to CN code 2204 30 92.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for CN 2204 30 92 states two discriminating criteria: (1) the grape must is "concentrated" and (2) it refers to additional note 7 to Chapter 22, which defines the threshold conditions for "concentrated grape must" (typically expressed as a minimum total dry extract value in g/l). Criterion (1) is captured via the new ConcentratedGrapeMustQuality restriction. The sibling class GrapeMustInFermentationOrArrestedWithoutAlcohol22043010 is excluded via the complement restriction on FermentationArrestedWithoutAlcoholQuality. Criterion (2) — the specific numeric threshold from additional note 7 (e.g., a minimum Brix or dry extract level) — cannot be fully captured because the text of additional note 7 to Chapter 22 is not provided in the legal notes supplied. This reduces the coverage score from 1.0 to 0.75. The TotalDryExtractQuality class is present in the TBox and could be used to encode the numeric threshold if the note text were available. Additionally, CN 2204 30 includes a third subheading (2204 30 96, other/non-concentrated), and the complement axiom helps discriminate from the fermentation-arrested must sibling, but the non-concentrated sibling (2204 30 96) is not yet in the TBox and no explicit complement restriction against it is placed here — this is a gap noted for completeness.
